title: pi-workflows expression evaluator has no determinism enforcement — expressions evaluated at dispatch time can include non-deterministic patterns (current-time / random / uuid) that break resume semantics silently; Claude Code workflows reject Date.now() / Math.random() / argless new Date() at validation time to maintain cache + resume integrity
description: Per R-0014 recon axis 12: Claude Code workflows throw at script-validation time on Date.now() / Math.random() / argless new Date() — would break the (prompt, opts) cache + resume semantics (same script + same args must produce identical agent results). pi-workflows expression evaluator (${{ }} syntax) lets operators reference any value at dispatch time; if expression evaluates to current time / random number / uuid / etc., resume re-evaluates → different value → resume cache (when added per FGAP-147) would never hit. Today no enforcement; no warning; silent non-determinism breaks resume idempotence.

evidence.reference: Part 3 axis 12 — Resume-Incompatible API Enforcement — INSPIRE verdict
expression evaluator with no determinism enforcement

impact: Resume semantics silently break when operators include non-deterministic expressions. Cache-by-input (FGAP-147) would never hit. State persistence + resume produce different agent dispatches than original run. Operators discover the gap only when resume yields different results — late + diagnostic-poor.
canonical_vocabulary: determinism enforcement; resume-cache integrity; non-deterministic-expression detection; pre-flight expression validation
proposed_resolution: Add pre-flight expression validator to validateWorkflow: scan all ${{ }} expressions for disallowed patterns (Date.now, new Date() with no args, Math.random, crypto.randomUUID, uuid(), or any user-registered non-deterministic filter). Issue warning at validation-time (status: warning) OR error under --strict-determinism flag. Documentation: enumerate which built-in filters are deterministic vs non-deterministic + which expression patterns to avoid. Optional: provide deterministic alternatives (e.g., 'workflow.startTime' filter that captures startup-time once + returns same value across resumes).
